title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 9 (Fragment 4)
text:
Judy was coming along the stage holding the white rolled-up shape of the baby. Judy wore a blue nightdress and a blue cap. Her face was mauve, with a nose in the middle of it nearly as large and red as Tonino’s. But the eyes on either side of it were Angelica’s, alternately blinking and wide with terror. She blinked beseechingly at Tonino as she squawked, “I have to go out, Mr. Punch. Mind you mind the baby!”

“Don’t want to mind the baby!” he squawked.

All through the long silly conversation, he could see Angelica’s eyes blinking at him, imploring him to think of a spell to stop this. But of course Tonino could not. He did not think Rinaldo, or even Antonio, could stop anything as powerful as this. Angel of Caprona! he thought. Help us! That made him feel better, although nothing stopped the spell. Angelica planted the baby in his arms and dived out of sight.

The baby started to cry. Tonino first squawked abuse at it, then took it by the end of its long white dress and beat its brains out on the platform. The baby was much more realistic than Dog Toby. It may have been only cardboard, but it wriggled and waved its arms and cried most horribly. Tonino could almost have believed it was Cousin Claudia’s baby. It so horrified him that he found he was repeating the words of the Angel of Caprona as he swung the baby up and down. And those might not have been the right words, but he could feel they were doing something. When he finally flung the white bundle over the front of the stage, he could see the shiny floor the baby fell on, away below. And when he looked up at the clapping spectators, he could see them too, equally clearly.

The first person he saw was the Duke of Caprona. He was sitting on a gilded chair, in a glitter of buttons, laughing gigantically. Tonino wondered how he could laugh like that at something so horrible, until he remembered that he had stood himself, a score of times, and laughed himself sick, at just the same thing. But those had been only puppets. Then it dawned on Tonino that the Duke thought they were puppets. He was laughing at the skill of the showman.

“What a clever fellow!” squawked Tonino, and was made to dance about gleefully, without wanting to in the least. But as he danced, he looked sharply at the rest of the audience, to see who it was who knew he was not a puppet.

To his terror, a good half of them knew. Tonino met a knowing look on the faces of the three grave men surrounding the Duke, and the same on the elegantly made-up faces of the two ladies with the Duchess. And the Duchess—as soon as Tonino saw the amused arch of the Duchess’s eyebrows and the little, secret smile on her mouth, he knew she was the one doing it. He looked her in the eyes. Yes, she was an enchantress. That was what had so troubled him about her when he saw her before. And the Duchess saw him look, and smiled less secretly, because Tonino could do nothing about it.

vocabulary.definition: Deliberately or affectedly playful and teasing.
vocabulary.example: And the Duchess—as soon as Tonino saw the amused arch of the Duchess’s eyebrows and the little, secret smile on her mouth, he knew she was the one doing it.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that tries to persuade someone urgently or anxiously to do something.
vocabulary.example: She blinked beseechingly at Tonino as she squawked, “I have to go out, Mr.

vocabulary.definition: Shining intermittently or moving the eyelids quickly.
vocabulary.example: But the eyes on either side of it were Angelica’s, alternately blinking and wide with terror.

vocabulary.definition: A collection of things wrapped or tied together.
vocabulary.example: When he finally flung the white bundle over the front of the stage, he could see the shiny floor the baby fell on, away below.

vocabulary.definition: Making the sound of hands striking together to show approval or applause.
vocabulary.example: And when he looked up at the clapping spectators, he could see them too, equally clearly.

vocabulary.definition: Quick to understand, learn, and devise or apply ideas.
vocabulary.example: “What a clever fellow!

vocabulary.definition: Informal talk involving two or more people.
vocabulary.example: All through the long silly conversation, he could see Angelica’s eyes blinking at him, imploring him to think of a spell to stop this.

vocabulary.definition: Moved rhythmically to music.
vocabulary.example: ” squawked Tonino, and was made to dance about gleefully, without wanting to in the least.

vocabulary.definition: To move suddenly or quickly, especially downwards or underwater.
vocabulary.example: Angelica planted the baby in his arms and dived out of sight.

vocabulary.definition: In a graceful and stylish manner.
vocabulary.example: Tonino met a knowing look on the faces of the three grave men surrounding the Duke, and the same on the elegantly made-up faces of the two ladies with the Duchess.

vocabulary.definition: A woman who uses magic or sorcery, or one who enchants or fascinates.
vocabulary.example: Yes, she was an enchantress.

vocabulary.definition: To an extremely large degree or extent.
vocabulary.example: He was sitting on a gilded chair, in a glitter of buttons, laughing gigantically.

vocabulary.definition: Covered thinly with gold leaf or gold paint.
vocabulary.example: He was sitting on a gilded chair, in a glitter of buttons, laughing gigantically.

vocabulary.definition: In a joyful or triumphant manner.
vocabulary.example: ” squawked Tonino, and was made to dance about gleefully, without wanting to in the least.

vocabulary.definition: Serious or solemn in manner or appearance.
vocabulary.example: Tonino met a knowing look on the faces of the three grave men surrounding the Duke, and the same on the elegantly made-up faces of the two ladies with the Duchess.

vocabulary.definition: Causing horror; very unpleasant or bad.
vocabulary.example: It may have been only cardboard, but it wriggled and waved its arms and cried most horribly.

vocabulary.definition: Filled with horror or shock.
vocabulary.example: It so horrified him that he found he was repeating the words of the Angel of Caprona as he swung the baby up and down.

vocabulary.definition: Begging earnestly or desperately.
vocabulary.example: All through the long silly conversation, he could see Angelica’s eyes blinking at him, imploring him to think of a spell to stop this.

vocabulary.definition: Showing awareness or understanding of something secret or private.
vocabulary.example: Tonino met a knowing look on the faces of the three grave men surrounding the Duke, and the same on the elegantly made-up faces of the two ladies with the Duchess.

vocabulary.definition: Wearing cosmetics.
vocabulary.example: Tonino met a knowing look on the faces of the three grave men surrounding the Duke, and the same on the elegantly made-up faces of the two ladies with the Duchess.

vocabulary.definition: Having great power or strength.
vocabulary.example: He did not think Rinaldo, or even Antonio, could stop anything as powerful as this.

vocabulary.definition: A figure moved by strings or hands, used in entertainment.
vocabulary.example: But those had been only puppets.

vocabulary.definition: Representing things as they are in real life.
vocabulary.example: The baby was much more realistic than Dog Toby.

vocabulary.definition: Showing a lack of good sense or judgment; foolish.
vocabulary.example: All through the long silly conversation, he could see Angelica’s eyes blinking at him, imploring him to think of a spell to stop this.

vocabulary.definition: A person who watches at an event, especially a sports event.
vocabulary.example: And when he looked up at the clapping spectators, he could see them too, equally clearly.
